---
title: 'Homework 7'
author: "Frank Marino"
date: 'March 22, 2026'
output:
  html_document:
    theme: readable
---

> Canva and Tableau is not used for this assignment. There is also no presentation for this assignment.

## Set-up

> Homework 7 follows a tutorial for mapping spatio-temporal data using the `storms` data from the `dplyr` package. [Click here to access the full tutorial.](https://r-spatial.org/r/2017/08/28/nest.html)

### Load Libraries
```{r, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
# Load any R Packages you may need
rm(list = ls())
library(mosaic)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(sf)
library(mapview)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
rm(list = ls())

library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggplot2)
library(class)
library(caret)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE
)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
rm(list =)

### Load Data

You'll want to **change the names of the Hurricanes in Line 42** from `Sandy`, `Katrina`, and `Jerry` to 3 other hurricane names. Below is a code chunk you can run to view a list of the summary statistics for wind speed with the names of all the storms in this data set.
```{r, eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}
storm_names <- dplyr::storms
storm_names <- filter(storm_names, !grepl('AL0*', name) & !grepl('Al2*', name))
storm_names <- storm_names %>%
  select(wind, status, name)
favstats(wind ~ name, data = storm_names)
```

From this list, choose **THREE** Names to edit in the following code chunk in Line 42:

```{r, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
# Load Data
storms_data <- dplyr::storms
storms_data <- filter(storms_data, name == "Frances" | name == "Bret" | name == "Hortense")
storms_data <- filter(storms_data, status == "tropical depression" | status == "tropical storm" | status == "hurricane")
```

#### DO NOT EDIT THIS CODE CHUNK

```{r, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, echo=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# DO NOT EDIT
#For Exercise 1
storms_data.sf <- storms_data %>% 
  st_as_sf(coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4326) 
class(storms_data.sf)

storms_data.sf <- storms_data.sf %>% 
  mutate(time = as.POSIXct(paste(paste(year,month,day, sep = "-"), 
                                 paste(hour, ":00", sep = "")))) %>% 
  select(-month, -day, -hour)

storms_data.sf %>% select(name, time, geometry)

#Coding by Storm Status
cls <- storms_data.sf %>% pull(status)
cls <- factor(cls, levels = unique(cls))
col = sf.colors(length(levels(cls)))

(storms_data.nest <- storms_data.sf %>% group_by(name, year) %>% nest)
to_line <- function(tr) st_cast(st_combine(tr), "LINESTRING") %>% .[[1]] 
(tracks <- storms_data.nest %>% pull(data) %>% map(to_line) %>% st_sfc(crs = 4326))
(storms_data.tr <- storms_data.nest %>% select(-data) %>% st_sf(geometry = tracks))

#For Exercise 2
to_lines <- function(tr) { 
  g = st_geometry(tr)
  hd = head(g, -1)
  tl = tail(g, -1)
  map2(hd, tl, function(x,y) st_combine(st_sfc(x, y, crs = 4326))) %>% 
    map(function(x) st_cast(x, "LINESTRING"))
}

trs <- storms_data.nest %>% 
  pull(data) %>% 
  map(to_lines) %>% 
  unlist(recursive = FALSE) %>% 
  do.call(c, .)

fn = function(x) head(x, -1) %>% as.data.frame %>% select(-geometry)
storms_data.nest <- storms_data.nest %>% mutate(data = map(data, fn))

storms_data.tr2 <- storms_data.nest %>% unnest %>% st_set_geometry(trs)
nrow(storms_data.tr2)
```

* * *

## Exercise 1
### (a)
Create a base R plot that maps the tracks of the storms using longitude and latitude, while color coding by **storm status**.

```{r}
storms.sf <- storms_data %>% 
  st_as_sf(coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4326) 
class(storms.sf)
storms.sf %>% select(name, year, geometry)
storms.sf <- storms.sf %>% 
  mutate(time = as.POSIXct(paste(paste(year,month,day, sep = "-"), 
                                 paste(hour, ":00", sep = "")))) %>% 
  select(-month, -day, -hour)
storms.sf %>% select(name, time, geometry)
status_colors <- c(
  "hurricane"           = "#D85A30",
  "tropical storm"      = "#1D9E75",
  "tropical depression" = "#4488DD"
)

# Map each point's status to the correct color
point_colors <- status_colors[as.character(storms.sf$status)]

plot(st_geometry(storms.sf), 
     cex     = .5, 
     pch     = 19, 
     axes    = TRUE, 
     graticule = TRUE, 
     col     = point_colors)

legend("topleft", 
       legend = names(status_colors), 
       col    = status_colors, 
       pch    = 19,
       bty    = "n")
```

### (b)
Create a plot using `ggplot()` that maps the tracks of the storms using longitude and latitude, while color coding by **storm name**.

```{r}
(storms.nest <- storms.sf %>% group_by(name, year) %>% nest)
to_line <- function(tr) st_cast(st_combine(tr), "LINESTRING") %>% .[[1]] 
(tracks <- storms.nest %>% pull(data) %>% map(to_line) %>% st_sfc(crs = 4326))
(storms.tr <- storms.nest %>% select(-data) %>% st_sf(geometry = tracks))
storms.tr %>% 
  ggplot(aes(color = name)) + geom_sf() + theme(legend.position="none")

```

### (c)
Create a plot using `ggplot()` that maps the tracks of the storms using longitude and latitude, while color coding by **year**.

```{r}
storms.tr %>% ggplot(aes(color = year)) + geom_sf()

```

### (d)
Create a plot using `mapview()` that maps the tracks of the storms, while color coding by **year**.

```{r}
library(mapview)
mapview(storms.tr, zcol = "year", legend = TRUE)
```


* * *

## Exercise 2

### (a)
Create a plot using `ggplot()` that maps the tracks of the storms using longitude and latitude, while color coding by **wind**.

```{r}
to_lines <- function(tr) { 
  g = st_geometry(tr)
  hd = head(g, -1)
  tl = tail(g, -1)
  map2(hd, tl, function(x,y) st_combine(st_sfc(x, y, crs = 4326))) %>% 
    map(function(x) st_cast(x, "LINESTRING"))
}
trs <- storms.nest %>% 
  pull(data) %>% 
  map(to_lines) %>% 
  unlist(recursive = FALSE) %>% 
  do.call(c, .)
fn = function(x) head(x, -1) %>% as.data.frame %>% select(-geometry)
storms.nest <- storms.nest %>% mutate(data = map(data, fn))
storms.tr2 <- storms.nest %>% unnest %>% st_set_geometry(trs)
nrow(storms.tr2)
## [1] 9584
storms.tr2 %>% ggplot(aes(color = wind)) + geom_sf()
```


### (b)
Create a plot that maps the tracks of the storms using `mapview()`, while color coding by **wind**.

```{r}
mapview(storms.tr2, zcol = "wind", legend = TRUE)

```

* * *

## Exercise 3

### (a)
Now create a new subset to filter on the data to have time greater than January 1, 2010.
```{r}
storms.tr3 <- storms.tr2 %>% 
  filter(time >= as.POSIXct("2010-01-01")) 
mapview(storms.tr3, zcol = "wind", lwd = 4)
```

### (b)
Create a plot using `ggplot()` that maps the tracks of the storms using longitude and latitude, while color coding by **wind**.

```{r}
plot(storms.tr3["wind"], type = 'p', cex = .2, pch = 16, axes = TRUE,
     graticule = TRUE)
plot(storms.tr3["wind"], type = 'l', add = TRUE)
```


### (c)
Create a plot using `mapview()` that maps the tracks of the storms using, while color coding by **wind**.

```{r}
mapview(storms.tr3, zcol = "wind", lwd = 4, legend = TRUE) +
  mapview(st_cast(storms.tr3, "POINT"), zcol = "wind", cex = 4)
```

* * * 

## All done!

Knit the completed R Markdown file as a HTML document (click the "Knit" button at the top of the script editor window) and upload it to the submission portal on Canvas.
